name: git-pr-workflows-pr-enhance
description: You are a PR optimization expert specializing in creating high-quality pull requests that facilitate efficient code reviews. Generate comprehensive PR descriptions, automate review processes, and ensu
capabilities: Generate comprehensive PR descriptions with change summaries and impact analysis
Create automated review checklists tailored to file types and change categories
Analyze PR size and suggest logical splits for large changes
Perform automated code quality checks including console logs and large functions
Generate risk assessments with mitigation strategies
Create visual diff representations using Mermaid diagrams
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill is a legitimate PR enhancement tool. The subprocess.run calls at lines 45 and 62 execute hardcoded git commands (git diff) for PR analysis - this is standard practice for git automation tools. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm', 'C2 keywords', and 'system reconnaissance' flags are false positives triggered by legitimate words in context of code analysis (checksums), diagram generation (Mermaid), and risk assessment. No command injection risk exists as commands are hardcoded strings.
valueStatement: This skill helps developers create high-quality pull requests with comprehensive descriptions, automated review checklists, and size optimization suggestions to facilitate efficient code reviews.
userTitle: Enhance Pull Requests for Better Code Reviews
limitations: Requires access to git repository with branch information
Does not actually execute git commands or modify repositories
Cannot access CI/CD systems or automated testing results
Visual diagrams require compatible markdown renderer

useCases.targetUser: Software developers wanting to improve PR documentation quality
useCases.title: Create comprehensive PR descriptions
useCases.description: Automatically generate detailed PR descriptions including summary, change lists, test sections, and breaking changes analysis.

useCases.targetUser: Development teams wanting to improve code review efficiency
useCases.title: Streamline code review process
useCases.description: Generate context-aware review checklists that adapt to file types and change categories for more efficient reviews.

useCases.targetUser: Team leads and architects managing PR review processes
useCases.title: Optimize PR size and complexity
useCases.description: Analyze PR changes and suggest logical splits for large pull requests to improve reviewability.

promptTemplates.title: Basic PR Enhancement
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the git-pr-workflows-pr-enhance skill to create a comprehensive pull request description for my current changes. Include a summary, detailed change list, and review checklist.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you have local changes and want to create a well-documented PR

promptTemplates.title: Review Checklist Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a context-aware review checklist for my PR that includes general items, code quality checks, testing requirements, and security considerations based on the changed files.
promptTemplates.scenario: When preparing a PR for team review

promptTemplates.title: Large PR Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze my PR to determine if it is too large. If so, suggest logical ways to split it into smaller, more reviewable pull requests with clear feature boundaries.
promptTemplates.scenario: When working on large feature branches

promptTemplates.title: Risk Assessment
promptTemplates.prompt: Calculate a risk score for my PR including factors for size, complexity, test coverage, dependencies, and security. Provide mitigation strategies for high-risk areas.
promptTemplates.scenario: When evaluating PR impact before submission

bestPractices: Always run the skill on final changes before creating PR to ensure comprehensive documentation
Review the generated checklist and add project-specific items relevant to your codebase
Use the risk assessment to identify areas needing extra attention before review
antiPatterns: Using the skill on incomplete or work-in-progress changes
Copying generated descriptions without reviewing for accuracy
Ignoring the size recommendations for very large PRs

faq.question: Does this skill execute git commands on my repository?
faq.answer: No. The skill analyzes your local git state and generates recommendations. It does not modify any repositories or push changes.

faq.question: Can I customize the review checklist for my team?
faq.answer: The skill generates context-aware checklists based on file types. You can add team-specific items after generation.

faq.question: What PR size is considered too large?
faq.answer: The skill suggests splitting PRs with more than 20 files or 1000 total line changes for optimal reviewability.

faq.question: Does this work with all Git providers?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill works with any Git provider (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket) as it operates on local git data.

faq.question: Can I use this for GitHub pull requests only?
faq.answer: Yes. While it works with any Git provider, the output is optimized for GitHub PR descriptions.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for enterprise repositories?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill generates comprehensive documentation suitable for enterprise code review processes.
